currentFocus: PatchBench DTO helper-output and rebuild front-door chain-map wave is integrated on main; the next developer slice is bounded PatchBench/BinaryPatchesPage test hardening.

integratedWave.summary: Canonical integration adds DTO/model-output coverage for PatchBenchOnlineReadinessText, adds a compact rebuild front-door chain map, and refreshes front-door/state hygiene without relying on local campaign paths or unreachable worker commits.
integratedWave.localOverlayClassification: Maintainer-local Ghidra descriptors in LOCAL_LAB_OVERLAY.md remain intentional overlay exceptions, not campaign truth or release payloads.

currentTruth.patchBenchBoundary: PatchBenchOnlineReadinessText remains presentation-only. BinaryPatchesPage still owns OnlineMultiplayerReadinessService calls and UI control assignment. AppCore receipt construction, patch planning, safe-copy manifests/signatures, launch arguments, process decisions, patch catalog rows, byte data, music behavior, Host/Join availability, online readiness, runtime proof, and release packaging are unchanged.
currentTruth.testCoverage: PatchBenchOnlineReadinessTextTests exercises Build and BuildCompanionSession against public-safe DTO/model inputs and guards missing or stale reflected WinUI build output.
currentTruth.rebuildDocs: roadmap/rebuild-front-door-chain-map.md now routes static closure, proof-class boundaries, short aliases, selected active rebuild-support scope, and explicit non-claims.
currentTruth.frontDoorHygiene: Tracked state/front-door docs no longer carry this wave's local campaign paths; coordination policy uses a generic outside-repo campaign-root description and public signoff uses a private-root placeholder.

runtimeBoundaries.onlineMultiplayer: not player-ready; Host/Join remains disabled until required distinct-endpoint and source-bound copied-runtime proof exists
runtimeBoundaries.staticReClosure: static closure is not runtime, gameplay, visual, online, rebuild, or parity proof

currentTruth.hardPayloadBoundary: Hard payloads, screenshots/frame dumps, raw proof logs, copied executables, full Ghidra databases, secrets, .env* files, build output, package output, and local runtime caches stay out of git and release ZIPs.

activeSlice.name: patchbench-binarypatchespage-test-hardening
activeSlice.goal: Introduce a small shared UiTests helper for reflected WinUI helper-output loading and add a focused guard that BinaryPatchesPage continues routing online-readiness presentation through PatchBenchOnlineReadinessText without inline Host/Join or online-ready wording.
activeSlice.claimBoundary: test/modularity hardening only; no AppCore/service behavior move, Host/Join enablement, online action enablement, release, runtime, Ghidra, hard-payload, installed-game, original-BEA, patch-row, byte-patch, launch, safe-copy manifest/signature, music, gameplay, visual, rebuild, or no-noticeable-difference claim changes

currentCounters.catalogRows: 29/29 target specimen identity and policy metadata rows

validation.currentIntegration: Integration validation uses git diff --check, JSON parse, stale local campaign path scan, docs/link/repo safety gates, WinUI build, focused PatchBench DTO helper-output tests, patch-engine safety, hard-payload safety, and WinUI primary-lane validation.
validation.nextSlice: Use focused PatchBench/WinUI tests plus the relevant repo safety gates; rebuild WinUI before reflected helper-output tests.

nextSteps: Before the next write, verify live HEAD, origin/main, and remote refs/heads/main.
Harden PatchBench/BinaryPatchesPage tests with a shared reflected WinUI helper-output loader and a routing guard.

Keep package scripts, release publication, Host/Join, online readiness, runtime audio, gameplay, visual, rebuild, no-noticeable-difference, Ghidra, hard-payload, installed-game, and original-BEA mutation claims bounded to accepted evidence.
